overview: The Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) is a Government of India initiative under the Startup India Action Plan, launched in 2016 by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT). It aims to mobilize domestic capital for startups by investing in SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs), which in turn provide equity and equity-linked funding to startups across various sectors and stages. The scheme has a total corpus of INR 10,000 crore and is managed by SIDBI as the implementing agency.
objectives: Provide funding support for development and growth of innovation-driven enterprises
Enable access to venture capital for startups across stages and sectors
Support development of smaller Venture Capital funds with focus on early growth stage startups
Nurture innovative ideas and promote innovation-driven manufacturing and long gestation technologies
Contribute to the corpus of SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) for investing in equity and equity-linked instruments of DPIIT-recognised startups
Promote ecosystem development by earmarking up to 5% of returns for capacity building activities
Strengthen monitoring and evaluation through an Empowered Committee with inter-ministerial representation
eligibility: The Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) does not invest directly into startups. Instead, it contributes to the corpus of SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs). AIFs must raise the balance 50% or more of the stated fund size from other investors to be eligible for FFS contribution. The FFS shall have representation on the governance structure/board of the venture fund based on the contribution made. Startups seeking indirect benefits must be recognised as 'startups' by the Central Government under DPIIT notification.
benefits: Indirect financial support to startups through SEBI-registered AIFs that receive contributions from FFS. These AIFs invest in startups via equity and equity-linked instruments. The scheme supports a broad mix of sectors including manufacturing, agriculture, health, and education. Up to 5% of returns from FFS are earmarked for ecosystem development activities such as sensitization, workshops, capacity building, plug-and-play shared facilities, mentorship, and regulatory support. The Fund of Funds 2.0 includes a segmented approach targeting deep tech, micro VCs, tech-driven innovative manufacturing, and sector/stage agnostic startups.
financial_support: The Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) operates as a Fund of Funds, meaning it does not invest directly into startups but participates in the capital of SEBI-registered Venture Funds (AIFs). It contributes up to 50% of the stated daughter fund size, provided the AIF has already raised the balance 50% or more from other investors. The total corpus is INR 10,000 crore, disbursed in tranches over the 16th and 17th Finance Commission Cycles. SIDBI acts as the Implementation Agency (IA) for FFS 2.0, continuing its role from FFS 1.0. Funds are disbursed to AIFs based on due diligence and recommendations from a Venture Capital Investment Committee (VCIC) constituted by DPIIT.
application_process: 1. AIFs submit proposals to the Implementation Agency (IA), which includes SIDBI and other selected domestic IAs. 2. IAs conduct due diligence on the proposals. 3. A Venture Capital Investment Committee (VCIC), constituted by DPIIT and comprising industry experts, subject matter experts, and IAs, evaluates the proposals. 4. VCIC considers AIFs managed by experienced professionals with proven track records for funding under the Scheme. 5. Upon approval, AIFs draw funds from FFS in tranches to meet the target corpus. 6. Post-investment, supported AIFs mentor and nurture their investee startups before exit or sale of investments.
deadlines: Commitments to AIFs spread over the 16th and 17th Finance Commission Cycles
implementing_agency: Small Industries Development Bank of India (SIDBI)
caveats: The Fund of Funds does not invest directly into startups; it invests only in SEBI-registered AIFs
FFS contribution is limited to a maximum of 50% of the stated daughter fund size
The daughter fund must have already raised 50% or more of its stated size from other investors
Up to 5% of returns are utilised for capacity building of the startup ecosystem and deposited back to the Consolidated Fund of India
The Scheme shall be known as 'Startup India Fund of Funds 2.0' and came into force from April 13, 2026
Commitments to AIFs are spread over the 16th and 17th Finance Commission Cycles
